articles.author: Adam Schreck, Melanie Lidman
articles.description: Iran has proposed a 30-day resolution aiming to end the war with Israel and the U.S. rather than just extend the ceasefire. President Donald Trump says he is reviewing a new proposal but doubts it will lead to a deal. Iran's 14-point plan calls for lifting sanctions, ending the naval blockade, and withdrawing U.S. forces. The proposal was sent via a Pakistani intermediary. Meanwhile, Iran remains firm on its stance regarding the Strait of Hormuz, with officials stating ships can pass after paying a toll. In Tehran, the rial continues to weaken against the U.S. dollar, causing economic instability.
articles.title: Iran proposes 30-day resolution to end war with United States

articles.author: Aniruddha Ghosal, Jim Gomez
articles.description: For millions of foreign workers in the Mideast, the Iran war has made life even more precarious. At least 28 were killed in missile and drone attacks before a fragile ceasefire took effect in early April. Now they face an even sharper version of a long-standing dilemma: Keep working in the Gulf, where wages are much higher, hoping that the truce endures, or return to already poor countries where prices have soared because of the conflict.
articles.title: Iran war sharpens the dilemmas faced by foreign workers in the Mideast

articles.author: Jamey Keaten, Ben Finley
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump’s pledge to shrink America’s military deployment in Germany is putting a new spotlight on the U.S. military presence in Europe. He said the U.S. would go “a lot further” than a reduction of 5,000 troops announced by the Defense Department. It is exposing a new rift between longtime NATO allies. Thousands of U.S. troops have been stationed in Europe since World War II to stabilize the continent and offer a bulwark against Soviet or Russian expansion. Here's a look at the U.S. military presence in Europe, how it might change, and how Germany has been adapting.
articles.title: What to know about US military presence in Europe as Trump seeks drawdown

articles.author: Elaine Kurtenbach
articles.description: Shares are mixed in Asia after U.S. stocks set more records, powered by strong profits for major companies. Oil prices held steady after U.S. President Donald Trump said the U.S. would help ships leave the Strait of Hormuz, starting on Monday. Iran has rejected the plan, but Trump also said talks with Iran could lead to positive outcomes. On Friday, the S&P 500 climbed 0.3% to another all-time high, closing out a fifth straight winning week. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dipped 0.3%, and the Nasdaq composite added 0.9% to its own record. Apple led the way after delivering better profit than expected.
articles.title: Asian markets are mixed and oil holds steady

articles.author: Gerald Imray
articles.description: The World Health Organization says that a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean has killed three people and sickened at least three others. In a statement to The Associated Press on Sunday, the WHO said an investigation was ongoing but that at least one case of hantavirus had been confirmed. One of the patients was in intensive care in a South African hospital, the U.N.’s health agency said, adding that it was working with authorities to evacuate two other passengers with symptoms from the ship. South African media reports said the outbreak happened on the MV Hondius cruise ship while it was sailing from Argentina to Cape Verde off the coast of West Africa.
articles.title: Three dead from hantavirus on a cruise ship in the Atlantic, WHO says

articles.author: Susan Montoya Bryan
articles.description: Three people have died and others have been sickened by what health authorities suspect is a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean. Hantaviruses exist throughout the world and are spread by contact with rodents. It's rare but also possible for a hantavirus to spread between people. In severe cases, the virus can cause a deadly lung infection or hemorrhagic fever with kidney failure. There is no specific treatment or cure for hantavirus infections, but early medical attention can increase the chance of survival.
articles.title: What to know about the suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship

articles.description: Police in an Oklahoma City suburb say that at least 10 people have been taken to hospitals following a shooting at party at a lake. Edmond, Oklahoma police spokesperson Emily Ward said authorities received multiple reports of shots being fired at a gathering of young people near Arcadia Lake at about 9 p.m.. She said that in addition to the 10 people taken to hospitals, more may have driven themselves. She said victims were in “various conditions.” Arcadia Lake is located in around 13 miles north of Oklahoma City in Edmond, a city of about 100,000. The lake is a popular place for picnicking, camping, fishing and water sports.
articles.title: Shooting at lake near Oklahoma City injures at least 10

articles.author: Michael R. Sisak, Christopher Weber
articles.description: A spokesman for Rudolph Giuliani says the ex-mayor of New York City and former Trump adviser is in critical but stable condition at a hospital. Spokesman Ted Goodman didn’t say what sent the 81-year-old to the hospital or how long he’s been there. Goodman on Sunday asked for prayers for the former mayor. Giuliani earned the “America’s Mayor” nickname for helping unite the nation following the Sept. 11 attacks. In recent years he became a divisive figure after serving as an adviser and personal lawyer for President Donald Trump.
articles.title: Rudy Giuliani in critical condition at hospital, his spokesman says

articles.author: David Fischer
articles.description: The first of 1,500 beagles being removed from a Wisconsin dog breeding and research business are adjusting to their new lives as animal welfare groups move them to shelters to prepare them for adoption. Ridglan Farms in rural Blue Mounds was the site of a violent clash last month between activists trying to break in and police who repelled them with tear gas and pepper spray. But the Big Dog Ranch Rescue and the Center for a Humane Economy had already been working for months to purchase the animals and find them better homes.
articles.title: 1,500 beagles find safety after protests at Wisconsin dog breeding site

articles.description: Ukraine has launched a wave of strikes against Russia’s oil export infrastructure, hitting a key loading port on the Baltic Sea and three tankers that Ukraine alleges were illegally used to transport Russian crude. A nighttime drone strike sparked a blaze at Russia’s largest oil exporting port on the Baltic Sea, according to Russian regional Gov. Alexander Drozdenko. The port of Primorsk lies over 1,000 kilometers (620 miles) from Ukraine. Elsewhere, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukrainian forces had hit two tankers in the Black Sea that had been used to transport Russian oil in violation of Western sanctions.
articles.title: Ukraine launches strikes on Russian oil infrastructure
